name: Championship Bacon-Wrapped Stuffed Shrimp
description: From Meathead: The Science of Great Barbecue and Grilling (Goldwyn), p.344. Large shrimp stuffed with cream cheese and jalapeno, wrapped in bacon, grilled two-zone, and glazed with Thai sweet chili.
recipeIngredient: 20 shell-on large (16/20 count) shrimp, preferably wild Gulf
Thai sweet chili sauce (e.g. Maggi Taste of Asia Mild Sweet Chili)
finely chopped chives or flat-leaf parsley (optional garnish)
recipeInstructions: Peel/devein shrimp leaving tails on; brine (p.343).
Halve and seed jalapenos; stuff each shrimp with cream cheese and a jalapeno strip, wrap in bacon, secure with a toothpick.
Grill two-zone (~325F indirect) until the bacon is done; brush with sweet chili sauce and garnish.
author: Meathead Goldwyn

ai.cliffNotes: Brining the shrimp before stuffing ensures they stay tender and juicy while the bacon renders and crisps over indirect heat.
keyTips: Brine the peeled shrimp first to season throughout and prevent them from drying out during grilling
Use the two-zone setup so bacon renders fully without overcooking the delicate shrimp meat
Secure each bundle tightly with a toothpick so the bacon stays wrapped and doesn't unroll during cooking
Don't skip leaving the tails on—they provide a handle and help shrimp cook evenly by keeping the shape intact
